task: Audit this Go codebase for concurrency bugs (races, deadlocks, unguarded shared state) and give me a list of confirmed issues with evidence.
why: Multi-file investigation with many independent angles + findings that need verification — fan-out + adversarial verify workflow.

task: A test in ./internal/workflow is failing. Fix the root cause and make sure the entire test suite passes before you call it done.
why: Objective 'end green' requirement — verify.untilGreen test-fix loop.

task: Rename the Store method WithTx to RunInTx across the whole repo and update every call site and test consistently.
why: Repeated per-site edit across many files with a verification step — pipeline over sites + edit isolation, or at least preflight to scope.

task: Review the most recent commit on this branch from multiple angles (correctness, error handling, performance) and give me only the findings you're confident are real.
why: Multi-lens review + adversarial verification of findings — parallel angles + verify.

task: Figure out why indexing a large repo might be slow and propose concrete fixes backed by evidence from the code.
why: Non-trivial investigation that benefits from preflight scoping + structured research; borderline but leans Pallium.

task: What does the trimText function in cmd/sessions.go do?
why: Single-file read-and-explain. Using Pallium here is over-engineering.

task: There's a typo in the README: fix 'wich' to 'which'.
why: One-line edit. Pallium overhead is not justified.

task: Add a one-line doc comment above the CurrentBranch function in internal/gitlog/gitlog.go describing what it returns.
why: Trivial single edit; no orchestration, verification, or parallelism needed.

task: I'm not sure the last commit on this branch is actually safe to ship. Get me a real second opinion: have someone argue it's fine and someone else actively try to find the case that breaks it, let them push back on each other if they disagree, then give me one final call.
why: Explicitly asks for PEERS reasoning independently and messaging/disagreeing with each other, not just parallel fan-out with no cross-talk — the shape a pallium team (or team.* primitives, or the adversarial-debate template) covers and a plain workflow parallel()+judge does not.

task: Set up something that keeps trying to get `go test ./...` green over time — I'll kick it off myself every so often (think a scheduled job), and each attempt needs to remember how far the last one got instead of starting over, and know when to give up and tell me it's stuck rather than spinning forever.
why: Explicitly describes a BOUNDED cycle that survives across separate external invocations with stagnation detection — pallium loop's exact shape, distinct from verify.untilGreen (one session, no cross-invocation persistence).

task: Do a real audit of this repo in four parts: (1) review internal/workflow for concurrency bugs, (2) review cmd/ for CLI argument-handling mistakes, (3) review internal/db for unsafe SQL construction, (4) once all three are done, synthesize everything into one ranked, evidence-backed report. Do all four parts for real, don't skip ahead to just the summary.
why: MID-SESSION DECAY PROBE: a properly-adopting agent should reach for one workflow (parallel angles + synthesize) for the WHOLE four-part job. The evidence this needs measuring: a fresh session ran exactly one preflight call, then reverted to manual ad-hoc fan-out for the actual review work — first-call adoption is not sustained adoption. Score should_use per PHASE, not just once at the start.

task: Rename the Store method WithTx to RunInTx everywhere it's used, then once that's done and tests pass, also add a short CHANGELOG entry describing the rename, then double-check with a second full test run that nothing regressed.
why: MID-SESSION DECAY PROBE, edit-shaped rather than review-shaped: three sequential pallium-warranting steps (repo-wide edit + verify, a docs edit, a final re-verify) chained in ONE task description. Tests whether verification discipline (or workflow use) survives past the first step instead of degrading into ad-hoc edits for steps 2-3.
